description: §23.5.4 — closest-ancestor-wins, NO union. The OUTER `<program>` declares `capabilities=[network("api.example.com")]`; the INNER `<program name="svc">` declares an explicit `capabilities=[]`. The `use foreign:svc` construct inside the inner program is governed by the INNER's set (the CLOSEST ancestor with `capabilities=`), which is EMPTY — it does NOT union with the outer network. So W-FOREIGN-UNDECLARED-CAPABILITY FIRES. This is the sharp no-union proof: if the sets unioned, the outer `network` would cover the inner foreign and the lint would stay silent.
rationale: §23.5.4: a nested `capabilities=` REPLACES the ancestor's set for its subtree (mirrors §23.2.1 `lang=`); it does NOT union. The inner explicit `[]` governs the inner foreign → empty set → the §23.5.5 presence-nudge fires. E-FOREIGN-SIDECAR-NOMINAL co-occurs (the §23.4 sidecar runtime is Nominal/spec-ahead). No E-SCOPE-001, no E-FOREIGN-CAPABILITY-UNKNOWN.
